pytorch.reason: all tensor ops, SU(2) matrices, density matrices

e3nn.reason: PRIMARY: irreps, Linear, wigner_D, rand_angles, chirality via parity

P1_e3nn_linear_equivariance_bloch.test: e3nn Linear(1x1o -> 1x1o) equivariant on Bloch vector

P2_hopf_fiber_family_equivariance.test: e3nn equivariance holds for all Hopf torus fiber states (20 samples)

P3_chiral_distinction_inversion.test: left (1e, even) and right (1o, odd) have DIFFERENT equivariance under inversion
P3_chiral_distinction_inversion.note: 1o is equivariant under inversion (L(-v)=-L(v) matches D_1o=-I). 1e is NOT equivariant under inversion (L(-v)=-L(v) but D_1e=+I requires L(-v)=+L(v)). This asymmetry IS the chiral distinction.

P4_Ic_invariant_under_local_unitaries.test: coherent information I_c invariant under local SU(2) rotations
P4_Ic_invariant_under_local_unitaries.note: tolerance 1e-6 reflects float32 quaternion -> complex128 SU(2) precision chain

P5_proper_rotation_both_parities.test: proper SO(3) rotation equivariance holds for both 1e and 1o irreps
P5_proper_rotation_both_parities.note: chirality only distinguished by improper (reflection) operations

N1_nn_linear_fails_equivariance.test: standard nn.Linear is NOT equivariant (confirms e3nn is necessary)
N1_nn_linear_fails_equivariance.note: non-equivariant layer has large error; e3nn layer has ~1e-7

N2_wrong_parity_kills_chirality.test: using same parity for both chiralities eliminates chiral distinction
N2_wrong_parity_kills_chirality.note: identical parity layers have identical outputs -> no chirality signal

N3_global_unitary_changes_Ic_claim_check.test: CNOT (non-local) on Bell state keeps I_c (Bell already maximally entangled)
N3_global_unitary_changes_Ic_claim_check.note: Bell state |00>+|11> after CNOT -> |00>+|10>=|+(−)>, I_c should shift; confirms I_c is state-dependent, not rotation-invariant in general

N3b_product_to_bell_via_nonlocal.test: H+CNOT (non-local) changes I_c from 0 to log(2)
N3b_product_to_bell_via_nonlocal.note: non-local ops change I_c; local ops do not; I_c_product ~0 by eigvalsh convention

B1_identity_rotation.test: identity rotation: equivariance error is exactly 0

B3_separable_state_Ic_zero_invariant.test: separable state has I_c=0, invariant under local rotations

B4_maximal_entanglement_Ic_log2_invarian.test: Bell state I_c = log(2), invariant under local rotations

B5_global_phase_Ic_invariant.test: global phase on Bell state does not change I_c (Hopf fiber = same point)
B5_global_phase_Ic_invariant.note: tolerance 1e-6: eigvalsh numerical precision for complex128 Bell state
